assertions.description: Output enforces zero formula errors requirement (#REF!, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, #N/A, #NAME?)
assertions.check: contains_one_of: ["zero formula error", "no formula error", "#REF!", "#DIV/0!", "formula error"]

assertions.description: Output references color coding standards for financial models (blue=inputs, black=formulas)
Output uses pandas for data analysis when reading spreadsheets
Output references LibreOffice or recalc.py for formula recalculation
Output does NOT use hardcoded values in formulas (use cell references)
Output references number formatting standards (currency, percentages, negatives)
Output preserves existing template conventions when editing files
Output length is under 600 words for focused responses

test_cases.input: Create a financial model spreadsheet for tracking monthly revenue projections with formulas for growth rates.
Read and analyze the data from an existing .xlsx file with sales data. Summarize trends and patterns.
I need to recalculate all formulas in a spreadsheet that was edited externally. How do I ensure formula integrity?
Full spreadsheet project: create a financial model with revenue projections, expense tracking, color coding, formatted outputs, and formula validation.
